resources.string: HR {bpm} bpm above {threshold} for {minutes} min — consider a break.
HR at {bpm} bpm (over {threshold} for {minutes} min) — slow down now.
HR drift {drift}% sustained {minutes} min — cool down and hydrate.
HR at {bpm} bpm — possible medical episode, check on the rider.
Emergency SOS button. Tap to trigger a 30-second countdown before alerting your emergency contacts.
Check-in timer. Tap to reset. If timer expires without check-in, your contacts will be alerted.
Custom message button. Tap to send your configured message instantly to your contacts.
Webhook action button. Tap to fire the configured HTTP request (garage door, smart home, etc.).

When OFF the extension stops monitoring everything — crash, speed-drop, check-in, Health (HR), Fueling. Notifications already configured (ride start/end, custom messages, webhooks) are also suppressed. Leave ON unless you want a hard stop.

Writes KSafe data into the recorded FIT so Strava / Intervals.icu / TrainingPeaks can plot it alongside HR and power. Includes cumulative carbs logged (g), cumulative carbs burned (g), current carb burn rate (g/h) and hydration (ml); plus, when wellness is enabled, current HR drift (%), max drift this ride, and wellness alert count.
This sends the configured emergency message through your active provider — no countdown, no cancel. Use this only to verify the channel works end-to-end.
Carbs use HR/power zones if available for intensity-aware targeting; without sensors the target is time-proportional. Hydration is always time-proportional — adjust target manually for hot days.
Emergency-level uses the urgent crash countdown beep — not configurable by design.
In Warning mode this alert plays the picker below (shared with wellness — one sound for every Warning-level alert).
Write KSafe data to FIT (Strava / Intervals.icu)
Reads the last 10 rides\' wellness summaries and shows a brief InRideAlert at the start of the next ride if the rider should take it easy. Silent when fully recovered — no per-ride spam. Default ON.

Target now varies with HR / power, body weight, ambient temperature and humidity. Uses karoo-headwind weather data when the extension is installed; otherwise the Karoo onboard temperature sensor (biased +3-8 °C) and 50 %% assumed humidity. Expect ±25-35 %% accuracy in the common HR + onboard configuration; closer to ±20 %% with power meter + Headwind.
Currently using the fixed per-hour target below.
Behind by {deficit}g — eat something now (target {target} g/h).
{elapsed} min since last log — time to eat (target {target} g/h).
{elapsed} min since last log — drink (target {target} ml/h).
Tap to log a configured carb item (e.g. gel, bar). Counts toward your hourly target.
Shows current carb deficit relative to target. Color-coded by urgency.
Current carb burn rate in g/h, modulated by your HR/power intensity zone. Falls back to the configured base target when no sensor data is available.
Cumulative carbs burned this session in grams (zone-aware integrated target). Companion field to Carb Status (deficit).
Tap to log a configured drink item (e.g. sip, bottle). Counts toward your hourly target.
Shows current hydration deficit relative to target. Color-coded by urgency.
Pair a HR sensor to enable medical and wellness detection. Without HR data these detectors stay idle.
Detects sudden heart-rate flatline or collapse during a ride. Requires a paired HR sensor.
Warns when HR stays above the threshold for a sustained period. Sent on-screen only — never to emergency contacts unless you change the response level.
Use % of max HR (from Karoo profile)
Requires a paired power meter. Compares current HR/W ratio against the baseline established in the first 10 min of each ride.
Credentials are saved per provider. Only the selected provider will be used to send alerts.
WhatsApp via CallMeBot. Free — up to 3 recipients; each one must activate their own API key from their own phone. Recipients 2 and 3 are optional.
Push notification via Pushover. Free 30-day trial, then ~$5 one-time. Enter your app token and user key.
Free unlimited push via ntfy.sh. No account needed — just pick a unique topic name and subscribe in the ntfy app.
Free Telegram messages via a bot. Create a bot with @BotFather, get the token, and enter your Chat ID.
Use {location} for GPS link and {reason} for cause

⛰ MTB · Gravel · Enduro. Requires a very hard impact (~5.5g) to trigger. MTB jump landings and drops rarely exceed this, so false positives from rough terrain are minimal. Use if you experience unwanted alerts on technical or rocky terrain.

🚴 Road + MTB mixed (recommended default). Balanced threshold (~4.5g) — detects most real crashes while tolerating normal road bumps and moderate trail impacts. Good starting point for any discipline. Min. speed 10 km/h.

🏁 Smooth road only (velodrome, closed circuit, bike path). Lower threshold (~3.5g) — more sensitive, detects lighter crashes. Safe to use on perfectly smooth surfaces where normal riding never generates large impacts. Do NOT use on MTB or rough roads — frequent false positives. Min. speed 15 km/h.

🔧 Custom threshold. Set the exact impact value with the slider below.
• Higher value → harder impact required → fewer false positives
• Lower value → detects lighter impacts → more sensitive
Useful if none of the presets match your riding style (e.g. aggressive enduro, triathlon on smooth roads).
Min. speed to detect crash (km/h, 0 = always)
MTB/gravel: 3 km/h recommended — crashes on technical climbs can happen at walking pace. Set to 0 to detect at any speed (more false positives when handling the bike).
Road + MTB: 10 km/h recommended — ignores impacts while nearly stopped (e.g. putting the bike on the car). Reduce to 3 km/h for MTB technical sections.
Smooth road: 15 km/h recommended — road crashes at very low speed are extremely rare. Set to 0 only for testing.
Choose based on your riding style. 0 = detect at any speed (useful for testing). Higher values reduce false positives when handling the bike at rest.

Speed below which the rider is considered stopped after an impact. Default 5 km/h. Increase to 8 km/h for MTB/gravel where sliding after a crash is common. Lower values (3) are stricter and reduce false positives on slow climbs.
Keeps crash detection active even when no ride is recording. Uses the minimum speed configured above. Useful if you want protection while doing a warm-up or a quick spin without starting a recording.
Monitor crash when not riding — any speed

Same as above but detects crashes at any speed, even while stationary (minimum speed is ignored). ⚠ This can produce more false positives — picking up or dropping the device while still may trigger an alert.
⚠ Crash monitoring is active outside of a recorded ride. The device must be moving at the minimum speed configured above (%1$d km/h).
⚠ Crash monitoring is active outside of a recorded ride at ANY speed, including while stationary. This increases the risk of false positives.

Sends a message when the ride starts (first start only — resuming after a pause does NOT send it again). Requires a Karoo Live key to include the tracking link; leave the key empty to send a plain message without a link.
Only the key part of the URL — e.g. from https://dashboard.hammerhead.io/live/3738Ag enter 3738Ag. Leave empty to send without a tracking link.
Use {livetrack} to insert the live tracking link (only included if a key is configured). You can also use {livetrack} in your emergency message.
Message sent when the ride recording stops completely. Does not require a Karoo Live key.
SOS in %1$ds — Tap field to cancel (%2$s)
Emergency alert sent to your contacts!
Check-in required in %1$d min — Tap timer field!
KSafe needs \"Draw over other apps\" permission to show the SOS cancel overlay on top of the ride screen. Without it, you won\'t see the cancel button during an emergency countdown.
Short name shown on the Karoo field button (e.g. "OK👍", "HOME", "SAFE")
Tap fields on the Karoo screen to send each message instantly (no countdown). Message 1 is also assignable to a hardware button.

Trigger any HTTP endpoint from a Karoo hardware button. Works with Home Assistant, ntfy, IFTTT, n8n, Make, or any webhook. The request is sent via the Karoo network bridge — works over Bluetooth tether when not on Wi-Fi.
The action will be blocked if the device is further than the configured radius from the target point. Useful to prevent triggering your garage door when away from home.
📍 Use current GPS location as target
Action blocked if device is further than this distance from the target
This message will appear as an on-screen notification every time the action fires

When enabled, KSafe records anonymous sensor data (accelerometer values, speed readings, detection states) to help the developer calibrate the crash detection algorithm. No GPS coordinates, no messages, no personal information of any kind is ever included. Data is sent automatically to the developer when you disable this option or finish a ride. Disabled by default — completely optional.
